records.ety: [L. circumstantia, fr. circumstans, -antis, p. pr. of circumstare to stand around; circum + stare to stand. See Stand.]

defs: That which attends, or relates to, or in some way affects, a fact or event; an attendant thing or state of things.
quotes: The circumstances are well known in the country where they happened.

defs: An event; a fact; a particular incident.
quotes: The sculptor had in his thoughts the conqueror weeping for new worlds, or the like circumstances in history.
So without more circumstance at all I hold it fit that we shake hands and part.

defs: Condition in regard to worldly estate; state of property; situation; surroundings.
quotes: When men are easy in their circumstances, they are naturally enemies to innovations.

defs: To place in a particular situation; to supply relative incidents.
quotes: The poet took the matters of fact as they came down to him and circumstanced them, after his own manner.
